name: cost-optimization
description: Optimize cloud costs through resource rightsizing, tagging strategies, reserved instances, and spending analysis. Use when reducing cloud expenses, analyzing infrastructure costs, or implementing cost governance policies.
capabilities: Provide AWS cost optimization strategies including Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and Spot Instances
Recommend Azure cost management approaches using Reserved VM Instances and Hybrid Benefit
Suggest GCP committed use discounts and sustained use optimizations
Guide implementation of cost allocation tagging strategies
Create Terraform configurations for S3 lifecycle policies and auto-scaling
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate cloud cost optimization guide containing documentation and Terraform configuration examples. All 30 static findings are false positives: backtick code blocks in markdown were misidentified as shell commands, AWS service names (EC2, Fargate, Lambda) were misidentified as C2 keywords, and infrastructure configurations were misidentified as system reconnaissance. No actual code execution, malware, or security threats present.
valueStatement: Reduce cloud spending across AWS, Azure, and GCP through resource rightsizing, reserved instances, and cost governance strategies.
limitations: Does not execute actual cloud API calls or modify resources
Cannot access real cloud account billing data
Does not provide real-time cost monitoring
Examples are illustrative and require customization for production use

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Reducing Cloud Bills
useCases.description: Analyze current infrastructure and recommend rightsizing, reserved instances, and auto-scaling configurations to reduce monthly cloud spending by 30-50%.

useCases.title: Finance Team Budget Management
useCases.description: Implement cost allocation tags, budget alerts, and spending dashboards to provide visibility and governance over cloud expenditures.

useCases.title: Multi-Cloud Strategy Optimization
useCases.description: Evaluate and optimize costs across AWS, Azure, and GCP using committed use discounts, spot instances, and managed services.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cost Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my cloud infrastructure for cost optimization opportunities. We use [AWS/Azure/GCP] and spend approximately $[amount] per month. What are the top 5 ways to reduce our cloud costs?
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with cloud cost optimization

promptTemplates.title: Reserved Instance Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me develop a Reserved Instance strategy for our AWS production workloads. We have 50 EC2 instances running 24/7 with consistent utilization. Which instance types should we consider and what commitment level makes sense?

promptTemplates.title: Tagging Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive tagging strategy for our cloud resources. Include recommended tag keys, values, and enforcement mechanisms. We need to track costs by environment, team, and application.

promptTemplates.title: Storage Cost Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Optimize our cloud storage costs. We have approximately 10TB of data in S3 that is accessed infrequently after 30 days. Design a lifecycle policy and recommend appropriate storage classes.

bestPractices: Start with visibility: Implement cost allocation tags before optimizing
Use a tiered approach: Reserved capacity for baseline, spot for variable workloads
Review costs weekly: Set up budget alerts at 80% threshold
Automate cleanup: Delete unused resources automatically
antiPatterns: Using On-Demand instances for predictable steady-state workloads
Not tagging resources - losing visibility into cost attribution
Ignoring storage lifecycle policies - paying for expensive hot storage

faq.question: Does this skill execute changes in my cloud account?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance, recommendations, and Terraform configuration examples. It does not make any actual API calls or modify your cloud resources.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers AWS, Azure, and GCP. It provides provider-specific strategies for Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and cost management tools.

faq.question: How much can I save with these optimizations?
faq.answer: Savings vary by workload. Typical savings range from 30-50% for general workloads, up to 70-90% for interruptible batch jobs using spot instances.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to implement these recommendations?
faq.answer: Yes. You will need appropriate IAM permissions for your cloud provider. For AWS: Cost Explorer, Compute Optimizer read access. For Azure: Cost Management reader. For GCP: Billing viewer.

faq.question: Can this skill help with multi-cloud cost optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers strategies for each provider and includes guidance on multi-cloud cost governance and comparison.

faq.question: What tools does this skill recommend for cost monitoring?
faq.answer: Provider-native tools: AWS Cost Explorer, Azure Cost Management, GCP Recommender. Third-party: CloudHealth, CloudAbility, Kubecost for Kubernetes.
